result.prompt:
Use image 1 as the exact single-frame idle keyframe structure source. Use image 2 as an enlarged full-body structure reference derived from the same original keyframe. Image 2 is not a new design source. It only reinforces the original anatomy, gaze direction, robe hem height, lower-body visibility, and full-body silhouette at a larger readable scale.

Keep the exact pose, facing direction, gaze direction, head angle, shoulder line, hand placement, pelvis position, leg spacing, knee height, calf width, boot placement, and robe hem placement from the original keyframe. Preserve the full visible body from hair to boots. Keep the exact composition, empty padding, and center alignment of the original source frame.

Restyle only the character design details for Dungeon Mage. This is the player character: a handsome blond teenage academy mage who entered the labyrinth for his graduation exam. He should read as a trained elite student mage, kind and intelligent, but distinctly sharper, cooler, and more disciplined than the previous results. Keep him youthful and noble, not dark or villainous. His body should read as lean and athletic, closer to a 174 cm / 64 kg trained student mage, slim rather than bulky, with light muscle definition rather than a heavy torso.

Push the face away from a round cute chibi read. Give him a cleaner narrower jaw line, less rounded cheeks, a more defined nose bridge, a restrained mouth shape, and longer sharper eyes with a stronger upper-eyelid line. The brow line should look focused and intelligent. He should still look like a good-natured student mage, but with a more intense, composed, elite expression. Do not make the eyes overly large, circular, or innocent-looking. Avoid a smiling or soft baby-faced expression.

His hair should be blond, short, and layered, with sharper strand separation and more angular silhouette breaks. Keep it close to the current runtime silhouette, but make the front and side sections more defined. Use swept-back volume with sharper layered bangs that partly frame the eyes instead of a round helmet-like hair mass.

The costume should be robe-driven and readable at small scale. The gray robe should dominate the outfit read and stay mostly closed, except around the neck. The robe should remain consistent and should not remove the original lower-body read. Do not add a belt, waist sash, waist strap, or any strong waist-cinching accessory to the robe. The robe should fall cleanly from the shoulders and torso instead of being tightly gathered at the waist. Only a slight hint of the blue outerwear and the white-and-gold inner collar should appear near the neck area. Preserve the original robe hem height and keep the same visible lower-body silhouette from image 1 and image 2. The lower body, legs, and boots must stay readable under the robe instead of disappearing into a single robe mass. Use robe folds that feel directional, angular, and disciplined rather than rounded and puffy. Shoes should be leather and harmonize with the robe palette. No gloves. The crossbody bag may remain barely visible because it sits mostly behind the body.

For this idle keyframe, the wand does not need to be visible in the hands. Keep the original hand placement and gameplay silhouette stable even when the wand is hidden from view.

Style: pixel-friendly 2D fantasy action RPG character, same visual family as Dungeon Mage monsters but cleaner, more disciplined, and more heroic. Lighting and mood: bright readable character treatment over subdued dungeon gameplay backgrounds. Background handling: transparent background, isolated single character keyframe. Important: preserve the original keyframe composition exactly and do not redesign the anatomy or pose.
